mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice risk downward in anticipation of reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134966.0 thousand as of Sept 2011
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS changes fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at elevated levels (above 4.5%) constrains credit availability and raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers. This monetary tightness depresses equity valuations and forward returns over a 60-day horizon as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand weakness that cascades into lower corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2120.0 thousand as of Aug 2011
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. Sustained weakness in INDPRO below trend signals supply-side constraints and demand insufficiency, reducing forward earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month INDPRO declines > 1.5% fail to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This weakens near-term demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as market reprices risk rapidly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: Monthly UNRATE increases > 0.2pp fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When inflation is rising and FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates compress equity risk premiums and forward returns become negative over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 199.1 as of Aug 2011, FEDFUNDS at 4.59% as of Sept 2011
regime_hint: stagflation risk or monetary policy constraint
failure_condition: Joint condition (rising CPI and elevated FEDFUNDS) fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the fundamental driver of long-term equity returns. Declining or below-trend GDP growth signals reduced future earnings capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized over prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11248.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2011
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth < 1.5% fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad-based economic momentum. When both decline simultaneously, it indicates synchronized supply and labor weakness that cascades into lower equity valuations and negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50 thousand in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.2088 and PAYEMS at 134966.0 thousand as of Sept 2011
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
